title: [Bug]: Unexpected result when `RIGHT JOIN` with subqueries
description:
<!-- A clear and concise description of bug. -->
Consider the following test case. The third query returns an unexpected result since the second query shows that the expression `(t1.c0 = t0.c2) IS NULL` is evaluated to `1`. Thus the result of the third query should return the row in the joined table.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (c3 VARCHAR(255), c4 INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (c3));
INSERT INTO t2 (c3, c4) VALUES ('test', NULL);
CREATE TABLE t3 (c5 FLOAT, c6 INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (c6));
SELECT * FROM t2 RIGHT JOIN (SELECT c3 FROM t2 WHERE c4 IS NULL) AS sub2 ON t2.c3 = sub2.c3 RIGHT JOIN t3 ON t3.c6 = COALESCE(t2.c4, -1);
key_bug_features: RIGHT JOIN with subqueries evaluating to unexpected results
